events.title: 🌈 SIX: The Musical - Opening Night
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
It’s time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown! 

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center! 

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX “TOTALLY RULES!” and The Washington Post hails it as “exactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!”

To enter:
Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those “reclaim your crown” moments! 

Two winners will be chosen to snag all 2 tickets each for the May 5th show. 

Show Details:
Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10) 

If you can’t make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We can’t wait to read your stories below. Let’s show them how Greensboro shines! 

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: 🌈 Lesbian Visibility Day
events.description:
🧡🤍💖 Today we celebrate Lesbian Visibility Day!!!🎉🎉🎉

A moment to honor the strength, love, resilience, and contributions of lesbian women everywhere. Visibility isn’t just about being seen, it’s about being recognized, valued, and uplifted in every space we enter.

As trailblazer Sally Ride once said:
“You can’t be what you can’t see.”

That truth still resonates. Visibility creates possibility. It opens doors for the next generation to live boldly, authentically, and without apology.

Today, we celebrate:
🧡 The stories that have been told, and the ones still waiting to be heard
🤍 The courage it takes to live openly and honestly
💖 The community that continues to build spaces of love, safety, and empowerment

To every lesbian who has ever questioned if they belong, you do. Your existence matters. Your love matters. Your visibility matters.

Let’s continue to show up, speak out, and stand proud, not just today, but every day.

#LesbianVisibilityDay #VisibilityMatters #Pride365 #GSOPride

events.title: 🌈 Community Walk, Mental Care in Motion
events.location: Greensboro Country Park, picnic tables near the Jaycee Park area
events.description:
🌿 Community Walk, Mental Care in Motion 👟💚

After days of ice, snow, and staying tucked inside, the weather is finally giving us a break, and so should we. Join us tomorrow for our Community Walk as we step back outside, breathe deeply, and reconnect as a community.

If your thoughts have felt noisy, heavy, or scattered after this stretch of bad weather, this walk is for you. Moving together, even gently, can help clear mental space and remind us we’re not alone.

✨ Ask yourself: What’s taking up my mental space right now?
Then come walk it out with us.

📅 Tomorrow
⏰ 10:00 AM
📍 Greensboro Country Park
👉 Meet at the picnic tables near the Jaycee Park area
🚶 Leisurely 2.5-mile route, walk at your own pace

All paces welcome. All identities welcome. Come as you are, let’s reset, reflect, and care for our minds together.

Hosted by IMPAQT GSO in partnership with Carolina Aging Alliance.

#Respect #Inclusivity #CAA #IMPAQTGSO

events.title: 🎨 Homebody by Kathryn Hood – Reception
events.description:
Move through Homebody, an act of resensitization🪑🧠

Join us TOMORROW for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood  from 6-8pm🥂

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 Garden of Corporeal Fantasy — Exhibition
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm — step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder — a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.

events.title: 🎨 Love would feel like this — MFA Thesis Exhibition
events.description:
Enter Love Would Feel Like This. The space unfolds with every step.🍂🐚

Join us TONIGHT from 6-8pm to celebrate the reception of Love would feel like this by Inga Manticas 🥂

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Love would feel like this
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Inga Manticas

Exhibition: April 14 -18, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 17th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours: 
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

If I had one wish …

Love would feel like this consists of drawings and sculptures that grew up together in my studio. It’s a forest, a river of new substances, a procession of characters with disputed and shifting identities, material marriages, corkboards of a conspiracy theorist, and whatever else might be attracted to this place. 

I am trying to communicate with beings whose spirits and material forms are very different from my own. Beings who we can feel but cannot see or name, who exist two places at once, who have hundreds of identities, who are the opposites of themselves, who contain all the rest of us. What practices and processes can put me in touch with plants, land spirits, gods, animals, hills, rivers, gems, gnomes, ghosts, past or lost selves, the numinous and ineffable? 

I wonder what I can say to them and what I can give them, and I try to hear their responses. I wonder, how can I love them? How can I feel their love back?

events.title: 🎨 Reception: Love would feel like this — MFA Thesis Exhibition
events.description:
MORE OF OUR MFA THESIS SEASON | Week 4! ✨And we are welcoming in Inga Manticas this week! Here’s a look into getting it all ready, be sure to swing by to see for yourself! ✌🏼 

Join us on Friday, April 17th 6-8p for a celebratory reception! 🥂

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Love would feel like this
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Inga Manticas

Exhibition: April 14 -18, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 17th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours: 
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

If I had one wish …

Love would feel like this consists of drawings and sculptures that grew up together in my studio. It’s a forest, a river of new substances, a procession of characters with disputed and shifting identities, material marriages, corkboards of a conspiracy theorist, and whatever else might be attracted to this place. 

I am trying to communicate with beings whose spirits and material forms are very different from my own. Beings who we can feel but cannot see or name, who exist two places at once, who have hundreds of identities, who are the opposites of themselves, who contain all the rest of us. What practices and processes can put me in touch with plants, land spirits, gods, animals, hills, rivers, gems, gnomes, ghosts, past or lost selves, the numinous and ineffable? 

I wonder what I can say to them and what I can give them, and I try to hear their responses. I wonder, how can I love them? How can I feel their love back?

events.title: 🎨 Nepantla: MFA Thesis Exhibition by Sofia Aguilar
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us last night to celebrate Nepantla by Sofia Aguilar🪞🪶

The exhibition is on view until 5pm today—don’t miss your chance to experience Nepantla.

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Nepantla
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Sofia Aguilar

Exhibition: April 7 - 11, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 10th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

An exploration of living between cultures, Nepantlaweaves together memory, myth, and lived experience to make the in-between visible.

Nepantla takes its name from a Nahuatl word meaning “in-between,” a space of transition and becoming that reflects my experience as a second-generation Mexican American navigating cultures, languages, and geographies. Raised within oral traditions and stories from my grandparents, I first understood Mexico as something carried inside the home; later travels to Mexico and an artist residency in the Netherlands expanded that understanding, deepening my awareness of hybridity and adaptation. Through printmaking, painting, and paper weaving, I build a vibrant visual language rooted in heritage and layered iconography, with woven works acting as metaphors for cultural transmission—interlaced, frayed, and resilient. Recurring figures such as the Tecuan from traditional dance and my skeletal cowgirl, inspired by Mexica cosmology, inhabit contemporary settings, collapsing temporal boundaries so that ancestral histories and present realities coexist, woven together in an ongoing search for belonging.

events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us last night to celebrate Mind’s Eye by Sammi Eady🥂🧵

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Mind’s Eye
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Sammi Eady scr4mmi 

Exhibition: March 31 - April 4, 2026
Reception: Saturday, April 4th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours: 
Tuesday - Thursday 12-5p
Closed, Friday April 3rd
Saturday, 2-5p

Mind’s Eye is an exhibition revealing the contention between how the mind and the body remember a space.

From childhood homes, first jobs, and card games, landmarks from my past are mapped through ongoing perception rather than fixed recordings. Relying on mind palaces and other mnemonic devices, both somatic and psychological approaches of visualization result in mixed perspective landscapes. Vulnerable fabrics bunch with memory. Artifacts extracted from these mappings indulge in their permeability.

Mind’s Eye is a metaphysical world in which memory is re-imbued into the blueprints of its infrastructures, embracing the distortion of perception that happens during repeated recollection.

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: 🎨 Advance Base & Moontype with likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th 👾🎶🎹

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We’re super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro’s own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as “lo-fi,” “depressed” & “weirdly uplifting.” Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy’s dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

events.description:

𝙈𝙖𝙥𝙨 features Brian Haran (Ama Divers, Filthybirds; engineer on numerous records) handling electronics/oscillators/tapeloops, Nick Vanbuskirk on sax (Panes, Kill the Buddha, bedroom division, and countless others), along with Tyler Monroe behind the drums (plays with too many people to count). This project is 100% improv—noisy ambient colliding with avant jazz. brian.s.haran likewindowpanes _tyler.monroe_ 

𝙇𝙪𝙠𝙚 𝙎𝙘𝙝𝙣𝙚𝙞𝙙𝙚𝙧 is a Nashville based pedal steel guitarist who has worked with a variety of artists including Margo Price, Orville Peck, S.G. Goodman, William Tyler, and Futurebirds. His debut solo album of ambient New Age music created entirely on pedal steel, ‘Altar of Harmony,’ was released by Third Man Records in 2020. His new EP ‘For Dancing In Quiet Light’ is out now via Leaving Records. lukefromnashville 

𝙎𝙖𝙖𝙥𝙖𝙩𝙤 is the music project of upstate New York–based sound artist Brendan Principato, who weaves manipulated field recordings with lush electronic soundscapes exploring ecology and music. He has held residencies with the National and Alaska State Park Services, including 2023 near Juneau and 2022 on Fire Island. His recordings have been named Best Ambient on Bandcamp, and his live performances include collaborations with Laraaji, Don Slepian, Patricia Wolf, and Green-House. saapato

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

jacobcmckelvie has traveled the DIY touring circuit in the US and UK for over a decade, carving out a niche for himself as a songwriter and performer. Known for his off-kilter and whimsical “stream of self-consciousness” lyricism and charming stage demeanor, he keeps audiences engaged both as a solo performer or with his newly minted backing band, “the New Old Band”. 

He released his latest LP “A New Kind of Hat” in 2024 and has been touring in support of that since.

Jake’s music has been featured on Americana UK, WFMU, Daytrotter, No Depression and has opened for acts like AJJ, Jeffrey Lewis, Clem Snide, Jeff Rosenstock and Steve Forbert.

“If you like a literate singer-songwriter with a witty turn of phrase then Jake is your man.” - Americana UK

events.title: 🎨 Crisis Actress_ and officialclairemusic Live Performance
events.description:
⛓️FRIDAY, OCTOBER 3rd⛓️DOORS at 8/SHOW at 9pm/$12 suggested/See ya at the show! 🤠

Chicago-based crisis.actress_ crafts experimental pop that exists in perpetual evolution. At the heart of their sound are swirling keyboards, layered vocal harmonies, loops, and motorik guitar lines that propel their performances into unexplored territories. Guests are frequent, though the core ensemble includes Brooks Hannon on guitar and electronics, Max Hudson on synthesizers and vocals, Sam Pardo on bass and vocals, and Jake Keenum on drums. Their full-length debut, Return of the Puppet King, captures the band’s shared musical obsessions and commitment to experimentation. Recorded at the hatlowed Electrical Audio studio last fall, each track serves as a testament to their collective journey a dream realized through collaboration with countless talented friends who helped shape their vision.

officialclairemusic is one of Chicago’s finest new songwriters. She works with calm confession, deep wit, and the details of a poet– all on top of big, swaying hooks. The Chicago Reader praised her “incisive, witty lyrics… a combination of sensitivity and straight talk.” She’s also a calm shredder of guitar– both gritty and delicate. Claire grew up in the Chicagoland area. Her music caught the attention of former Wilco member, LeRoy Bach. Bach is a member of her live band, (as well as Brooks Hannon & Jake Keenum from Crisis Actress.) Official Claire’s most recent studio offering, Midnight Dickbiter,features five remarkable new compositions.

patoiscounselors : Based in Charlotte, NC. Friends and fans of music making more music.
We’ve performed with Protomartyr, Gustaf, Ceremony, Automatic, Young Widows, BODEGA, Ekko Astral, Public Circuit, Truth Club, Quintron, The Messthetics, Omni, Sheer Mag, Ibex Clone, Algiers.

mostly_mesh : Bass heavy post-punk darkwave with campy references to 80’s horror, BDSM tinged lyrics, and the processing of trauma. Macabre love songs drenched in lust, sarcasm, and corn syrup blood. Mostly Mesh invites you to don your black lipstick and make out while a slasher plays in the background.

events.title: 🎨 DAGGERTEETH
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands—ink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: 🎨 LEAF LITTER Exhibition featuring 'Abalone Variation 1–6' by KT Wilson
events.description:
We’re thrilled to spotlight a truly captivating series in our current exhibition, LEAF LITTER—‘Abalone Variation 1–6’ by the talented artist KT Wilson. These original works are a celebration of the subtle, often overlooked wonders of nature, and we invite you to experience them in person at Irving Park Art & Frame.
Each piece in this series features a sustainably sourced abalone shell (pāua) from the pristine waters of New Zealand. Revered for their iridescent beauty and cultural significance, New Zealand abalone shells are harvested under one of the world’s most robust sustainability systems, ensuring that their natural populations are protected for generations to come. These shells, considered ‘taonga’ (treasures) in Māori culture, are not only a feast for the eyes but also a testament to the harmony of art and ecological responsibility⁠.
KT Wilson’s approach transforms each shell into a meditative focal point by mounting them in custom shadowbox frames. Rather than traditional fastenings, Wilson uses carefully woven textiles and thread to cradle each shell in place. This technique both secures the shell and highlights its natural form, color, and texture, drawing attention to the unique qualities of each specimen. The interplay between the organic shell and the geometric textile work invites viewers to contemplate the contrast and connection between the natural world and human creativity.
The ‘Abalone Variation’ series is a perfect fit for LEAF LITTER, our exhibition dedicated to the nuanced, often unnoticed aspects of nature. This show brings together artists whose work delves into the delicate, the intricate, and the ephemeral—reminding us that beauty is often found in quiet details. KT Wilson’s pieces exemplify this ethos, offering viewers a chance to slow down and appreciate the subtle complexity of natural materials and thoughtful craft.
.
Visit us at Irving Park Art & Frame to see KT Wilson’s work and explore the full LEAF LITTER exhibition. Support local art, sustainable materials, and the ongoing conversation between human hands and the natural world.
.
.
.
#ArtExhibition #ContemporaryArt #NatureInspired #KTWilson #AbaloneArt #SustainableArt #PauaShel

events.description:
INFINITY SONG 
ZIGGY’S GREENSBORO
FRIDAY OCTOBER 2

infinitysong Announces Self-Titled Album INFINITY SONG & Unleashes New
Single “One Foot Out”
International Tour Kicks Off April 27 Across U.S., U.K., Australia & Europe

April 24, 2026 – Today, sibling-powered soft rock group Infinity Song ushers in a new era with the announcement of their forthcoming self-titled album INFINITY SONG, arriving June 12, alongside the release of their vibrant new single “One Foot Out.” The track is available to stream now via Roc Nation Distribution. 

The 12-track album INFINITY SONG continues the band’s signature blend of soft rock, soul, and pop, expanding their sonic palette while maintaining the lush harmonies and warm instrumentation that have defined their sound. Across the project, Infinity Song leans further into emotionally direct songwriting, balancing timeless arrangements with an immediate, modern edge.
Alongside the album announcement, Infinity Song has released One Foot Out,” a pop-leaning indie track driven by bright guitars, punchy drums, and the group’s signature vocal blend. The song captures themes of indecision and emotional limbo, as the band sings, “Why don’t you say it like it is? Tell me
something real… You got one foot out, go ahead let the other one join it. If it’s not right now, it’ll never be again.”
In celebration of the new music, Infinity Song will embark on an international tour beginning April 27, with dates across the United States, United Kingdom, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. Known for their transcendent live performances and tight vocal arrangements, the band continues to build a reputation for transforming each show into an intimate, communal experience. 
Fresh off a whirlwind year of major milestones, Infinity Song has continued to build momentum following the release of their 2025 LIVE album, which captures the raw energy and emotion of their acclaimed performances.  The project features live renditions of fan favorites from their debut world tour and album Metamorphosis, including “Hater’s Anthem,” “Slow Burn,” “Sinking Boat,” and more.  downtowngso nclivemusictriad ohey.greensboro  tickets: ziggysgso.com

events.title: 🎵 Spencer Bland & John Thornton Comedy Show
events.description:
This Sunday the 3rd!
Doors at 6:30pm

Originally from North Carolina, Spencer Bland is a comedian and writer that wants you to know his last name has no effect on his comedy. Now based in LA, he has been featured on Netflix Is A Joke Radio on SiriusXM, the All Fantasy Everything podcast, and is a featured performer at comedy festivals across the country. As a professional dork, his dedication to useless things has led him to work on The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power on Amazon Prime. One fan from Wilmington described his comedy as “Moderately funny. Too much sex and too loud.” Come see for yourself!

John Thornton is a local standup comedian, a lover, a close, personal friend of Jay-z, and the director of a local non-profit that works with the homeless called Held. Some of this isn’t true. He’s been doing standup for over 15 years all over North Carolina and loves performing in Greensboro. In July of 2025, the non-profit he’s a director of hosted a comedy show fundraiser featuring SNL’s Andrew Dismukes and James Austin Johnson in downtown Greensboro.

events.title: 🎵 Ric Robertson and Dori Freeman Live Performance
events.description:
This Saturday the 2nd! Come get you some of this!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8:30pm

Ric Robertson crafts the kind of music that doesn’t beg for your attention — it quietly earns it, one note, one image, one breath at a time. A singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, and visual artist, he’s simply trying to make sense of the absurd beauty of being alive.

A gifted singer and songwriter whose music puts a contemporary spin on classic folk and country themes, Dori Freeman’s breakthrough in 2016 demonstrates why it’s not a bad idea to reach out to your favorite artists. Dori Freeman was born and raised in the Appalachian community of Galax, Virginia. Dori’s father, Scott Freeman, and her grandfather, Willard Gayheart, were veteran bluegrass musicians who played regularly and ran their own venue, Front Porch Gallery and Frame Shop. Dori picked up the guitar when she was 15 years old, and within a few years she was sharing the stage with the family band. After completing high school, Freeman attended Virginia Tech University, but she dropped out before graduating. By the time she was 22, Freeman was a single mother living with her folks, and she decided it was time she took her career seriously and put her talent to the test. Freeman started writing her own songs, and in 2011 she self-produced and released an album titled Porchlight.

events.title: 🎵 Matinee Performance with Martha Bassett
events.description:
This Sunday the 3rd!
Doors at 1pm
Show at 2pm

Come enjoy a special Matinee with Martha Bassett!

Martha Bassett is a Piedmont Triad–based singer, songwriter, and guitarist known for her expressive, warm alto and genre-spanning style that blends Americana, jazz, folk, and Bossa Nova. With more than two decades of performing experience, she has built a reputation for emotionally honest, intimate performances that connect deeply with audiences. A West Virginia native now rooted in North Carolina, Bassett is also the creator and host of The Martha Bassett Show, a collaborative live music series broadcast on NPR stations throughout the Mid-South and Pacific Northwest, highlighting national and regional artists.

events.title: 🎵 Fancy Gap & Josh Moore Live Performance
events.description:
This Friday the 1st!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8:30pm

Fancy Gap is the North Carolina duo of Stuart McLamb (The Love Language) and Charles Crossingham, blending country warmth, ’90s alt-rock energy, and heartfelt storytelling. Born from late-night writing sessions in their namesake Fancy Gap, VA, their debut album introduced a cinematic sound featuring Sharon Van Etten, Adam Lazzara (Taking Back Sunday), and Rami Jaffee (Foo Fighters/The Wallflowers), and was mixed by Grammy-winner Craig Alvin (Kacey Musgraves’ Golden Hour). Following a PBS Shaped by Sound feature and packed NC shows, the band released “Starlite Motel,” “Gold Coast,” and “Home Again,” featuring Daniel Womack of Futurebirds, with more singles leading into their sophomore album.

Josh Moore is a North Carolina–based singer-songwriter and former frontman of the influential post-hardcore band Beloved. After years in the heavier music world, Moore reemerged with a more intimate, soulful sound rooted in folk, Americana, and gospel traditions. His solo work, including Parted Ways and Sunrise, reflects themes of healing, faith, and personal transformation, delivered through warm, understated songwriting. A respected presence in the Carrboro music community, Moore has become a true “musician’s musician,” known for his deeply resonant voice and collaborative spirit.

events.title: 🎵 Laurelyn Dossett & Bet Williams Performance

This Wednesday, April 29
7:00 PM – 9:00 PM

It has truly been a pleasure getting to know Laurelyn over the past few months during her weekly residency with us. Her talent and authenticity are remarkable, and we’re excited to see what lies ahead with her upcoming release.

This Wednesday marks the final evening of the residency, and we look forward to closing it out on a high note. Join us for a special performance featuring Laurelyn Dossett and Bet Williams as they accompany one another on their songs.

We hope you can be part of this memorable evening—please feel free to share and help spread the word.

events.title: 🎵 Chris Acker Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 26th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 7:30pm

In a genre full of tall tales and marketable lies, New Orleans’ Chris Acker crafts candid songs – weaving his wit and woes into a body of work that exposes the stale plight of the American Songster to the honest, and sometimes hilarious, light of day.

In the tradition of Guthrie and Prine, Chris lends a quavering voice to the half-rotten romance of the unremarkable and unrefined. Regardless if you’re sitting by a backyard fire on a muggy Louisiana night or standing stage-side in some far flung town, when you hear Chris Acker playing, you’re hearing the end result of countless hours of observing the masters of the craft…and the end result of repeating their mistakes.

From the folk revival through the golden age of country music, deafened by punk shows and brass bands alike, Chris’s songwriting is a nod to the absurd yet muted brilliance that inhabits the molded corners of the bars he patrons and cratered street he treads, paired with a pained honesty that merits a long second look.

Acker released his fourth full-length album, Famous Lunch, on Gar Hole Records in October of 2024. Famous Lunch contains some of his best work yet: laid back, country-rockers like “Wouldn’t Do For You (Buddy)” and “Shit Surprise” (which Paste Magazine called a “song of the year contender” in 2024), a few raucous, bar-ready joints like “Don’t You Know (Who I Think I Am)” and “Bunn Machine”, and tender, folky triumphs like “Stubborn Eyes” and “Eyelash”, all showcasing Chris’ gift of building “vivid scenes at an impressively low word count” (No Depression). 

Acker tours solo or with his band The Growing Boys — Zach Thomas (bass), Nikolai Shveitser (pedal steel), Dave Hammer (guitar, vocals), Sam Gelband (drums, vocals), and occasionally Howe Pearson (piano).

events.title: 🎵 Wild Roots Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Saturday the 25th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Wild Roots is an independent, women-led band based in the Appalachian Mountains of North Carolina. Headed by Julia Houghton and Olivia Moore, the group released their debut album, Wild Roots Rising, in 2021 and have since continued to tour, playing stages and festivals across the country. Olivia and Julia have co-created music for nearly a decade and collaborate in many areas of life; in addition to being soul sisters, the two share a deep passion for sustainable living and environmental justice. While Wild Roots’ music celebrates the joy of friendship and community, it also seeks to hold those in power accountable and question the status quo. Julia and Olivia harness their political passions to shed light on issues of social injustice and the degradation of the natural world.

events.title: 🎵 Free Live Show Featuring Döltz and Instant Regrets
events.description:
We’d like to offer a heartfelt thank you to these incredible people. On Wednesday night, two local bands came together to host a free show that drew more than 100 vibrant, kind, and respectful members of our community. Choosing to make the event free opened the door for those who might not otherwise have been able to attend while also helping Abbey and me keep the doors open for another month.

They collected donations for the touring band, and their friends and fans showed up in full force, drinking, dancing, and creating an atmosphere that felt genuinely meaningful. It wasn’t just a show; it felt like a family gathering. It was community. It was a scene. It was punk in its truest form. 

We’re deeply grateful for these folks and all they do for us and this community. Thank you to every member of Döltz and Instant Regrets. We love y’all!

Josh & Abbey

events.title: 🎵 Murphy Campbell & Paper Wings Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Friday the 24th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Murphy Campbell has deep roots. Western North Carolina is a place her family settled centuries ago. Born in the foothills and coming from a long lineage of mountain folk - she seeks to draw up the remnants of a forgotten modal sound.

Long-time friends and collaborators Emily Mann and Wila Frank, known together as Paper Wings, dream up warm, pastoral folk songs suited to wandering through a forest or field, quiet contemplation, and long winding journeys. Furnished with delicate banjo and spellbinding harmonies so close you often can’t tell their voices apart, Frank & Mann deliver dynamic performances emboldened by the strength of their sincere songwriting. The duo have an uncommon ability to tastefully reference nostalgic sounds of American folk music while maintaining their own compelling style of artful and unpretentious lyricism. The strength and solitude one finds in the wilderness is a theme throughout their writing, and they lovingly transport listeners to open landscapes in which to find comfort and ask the questions which we all have in common.

events.title: 🎵 River Shook Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 23rd!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

rivershookforever w/ natehalltattoo 

River Shook is an artist in constant evolution. After a decade spent making their mark oncountry music, River Shook is closing the door on their first project (Sarah Shook & the Disarmers) and entering an exciting new era, debuting as a solo artist. River’s reputation for writing strikingly honest and transformative lyrics and their penchant for tapping elements of indie rock and pop music have forever cemented them as a country artist who exists to energetically push the boundaries of their genre into the future. River has delivered high-energy performances with the Disarmers at major festivals like SXSW, Hardly Strictly Bluegrass, Under The Big Sky, Pilgrimage, and the Long Road in the UK. They’ve supported Willie Nelson, Tyler Childers, Lucero, and the Indigo Girls. They’ve sold out headlining shows in venues from New York City to Los Angeles, from Barcelona to Stockholm, and many more.

events.title: 🤝 AAPI Heritage Month Cinema: Lupin the Third: The Castle of Cagliostro
events.description:
Our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series continues with a screening of "Lupin the Third: The Castle of Cagliostro" (PG), the classic anime film directed by Studio Ghibli founder and legendary filmmaker, Hayao Miyazaki, based on the manga series by Monkey Punch. 

After a successful robbery leaves famed thief Lupin the Third and his partner Jigen with nothing but a large amount of expertly crafted counterfeit bills, he decides to track down the forgers responsible—and steal any other treasures he may find in the Castle of Cagliostro, including the ‘damsel in distress’ he finds imprisoned there.

This film is presented in collaboration with the Manga Mania Book Club and is rated PG.

events.title: 🤝 AAPI Heritage Month Cinema Series: Whale Rider
events.description:
We are kicking off our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series next weekend with a screening of "Whale Rider" (PG-13), the award-winning film based on the novel by Witi Ihimaera. 🐋

A small Māori village faces a crisis when the heir to the leadership of the Ngāti Konohi dies at birth and is survived only by his twin sister, Pai. Although disregarded by her grandfather and shunned by the people of her village, twelve-year-old Pai remains certain of her calling and trains herself in the ways and customs of her people. With remarkable grace and courage, Pai summons the strength to both challenge and embrace a thousand years of tradition in order to fulfill her destiny.

📍 Hemphill Branch
📆 Saturday, May 2
⏰ 3 pm

events.title: 🤝 National Library Week
events.description:
The theme of this year's National Library Week is "Find Your Joy"!
 
“Find Your Joy” is an invitation for people of all backgrounds to explore and discover what sparks joy in them at the library. The theme amplifies a message that is ever-present in Mychal Threets’ viral videos about the innumerable ways people can find joy in the library and in his debut picture book, "I’m So Happy You’re Here: A Celebration of Library Joy."

⭐ Stop by your library and bring someone with you!
⭐ Help a friend sign up for a library card. It’s one small step that opens up endless possibilities!
⭐ Explore something new: a book, a program, a class, or a conversation. From storytimes to makerspaces, author talks to job support, libraries are places where curiosity and connection come to life!

"I love libraries enthusiastically every week! To serve as Honorary Chair for National Library Week takes that enthusiasm to a whole new level. Celebrating libraries, thanking library workers, visiting libraries is how I find my joy. There are so many library kids and library grown-ups who have yet to fully embrace their library joy, and I am so excited for them! When they enter the world of libraries and stories, I am confident they will find not only their library joy but their courage to believe in their own story and maybe even share it with others." —Mychal Threets

events.title: 🤝 The Women's Orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau: History, Myth, and Memory
events.description:
The women’s orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau, composed entirely of prisoners, was unique in the Nazi camp system. Dr. Susan Eischeid, who spent decades researching the orchestra and interviewing survivors, will present a history of the ensemble and dispel some of the myths surrounding it. She will also discuss conductor and virtuoso violinist, Alma Rosé, and founder, Maria Mandl, Head Overseer of the women's camp and one of the worst perpetrators of the Holocaust.

Dr. Susan J. Eischeid is an author, musician, and teacher, specializing in the music of the Holocaust. Professor Emeritus of Oboe at Valdosta State University, she has spent two decades researching the women’s orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau. She is the author of "Mistress of Life and Death: The Dark Journey of Maria Mandl", "Head Overseer of the Women’s Camp at Auschwitz-Birkenau", and "The Truth about Fania Fénelon and the Women's Orchestra of Auschwitz-Birkenau".

events.title: 🤝 Silent Book Club Greensboro Library Launch
events.description:
I’ve never quite read a book like Good People by patmeenasabit . It’s tangible and moving and makes you feel like you’re in the story yourself, listening to the retelling of the events that transpired in the Sharaf family. 

“This propulsive and provocative debut novel tackles the dark underside of gossip, examining the way it can shape and distort the truth until you’re not sure what is real or what you believe.”

It’s true, after every account I’d sit and question myself and my judgement. How does my perspective impact how I’m hearing the story? Can I separate myself from it? 

It’d been a while since I finished a book in a couple sittings. Wanna read it next? ✨

We want to start a little collection of books we take turns reading. A Silent Book Club Greensboro library, if you will. Check out a book, write the date and your name, let’s have fun with it and see where it goes. 

Good People by patmeenasabit is the first of our collection. Thank you to silentbookclub and crownpublishing for the gift! 

Have books you’d like to donate? Bring them to book club and we’ll add them to the collection! 

🎵: maiakmusic

events.title: 🤝 Release and Deportation of Cele Fierro
events.description:
"On the morning of Tuesday, October 7, the Argentine Embassy in Tel Aviv reported that Israeli authorities would transfer our comrade Cele Fierro along with other activists from Ketziot prison to the Jordanian border for release.

Our comrade Cele, who was aboard the Adara, along with two other Argentines, will fly out of Jordan early in the morning of Wednesday, October 8 (local time), to arrive in Buenos Aires later that same night.

We are proud of Cele’s internationalist militancy, putting her body on the line in the risky mission of the GSF, representing the MST of Argentina and the International Socialist League (ISL)." ligainternacionalsocialista cele_fierro
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Trial of Alejandro Bodart
events.description:
Palestine solidarity is not a crime, solidarity with Alejandro Bodart. At the end of this month Argentine socialist, Alejandro Bodart will be on trial once again for his solidarity and support of the Palestinian people and their resistance to genocide. Bodart’s trial is not only about Bodart, it is an international trend to criminalize any expression of solidarity with Palestine, while the State of Israel has murdered over 180,000 Palestinians. Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists stand in solidarity with Bordart and all those across the world resisting genocide. #absolucionparabodart
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Coalition to Reject A MAGA America Panel, Community Discussion and Organization Fair
events.description:
Come join the Coalition to Reject A MAGA America for a panel, community discussion and organization fair. Speakers from different local and international struggles will be speaking about their campaigns and organizational efforts to give local activists spaces where they can get involved in the struggle. A members Third Act will be speaking about how to stop the Transco SSEP pipeline, workers at REI and Reconsidered goods will be speaking on their unionization efforts, international speakers will be speaking on the struggles in Syria and Bangladesh, and activists in the October 22nd coalition will be speaking about the fight to end police violence. After the panel will be a community discussion followed by an organization fair where various different local orgs will have tables set up for participants to engage with. 
.
#organizethesouth #resist #wekeepussafe
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Special Meeting: 'Borders are prisons and prisons are borders' with Kristen Godfrey
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialism’s life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialists’ tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Community Demonstration Against Injustice
events.description:
Shout out to all the comrades and organizations that have been organizing this demonstration for months, thank you to all the comrades who raised their voices today against injustice and to call for unity in the community, in the class, and to call for solidarity. Today we just wanted to have a demonstration to tell the new administration that here in the Triad we are organized and that there is a resistance that will defend our rights and that if they touch one of us, we will all come out. We invite you, members of the community, whether you are part of an organization or not, who wants to collaborate, who is frustrated with what is happening and doesn’t know how to help, who want to understand and get more information, we invite you to ORGANIZE yourself by getting in touch with us or any organization that best suits you. We also want to make a call to the organizations that want to do things together to join us, we must open the channels of communication and support each other. We have many different tasks but the adversary is the same and in the end the fight is also for the same thing. So let’s organize, let’s fight together!  Please stay tuned for the next events, actions etc.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag
